Title: A multilayered cross-species analysis of GRAS transcription factors uncovered their functional networks in plant adaptation to the environment

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GRAS family transcription factor; GRAS domain (PF03514)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with regulation of seed saturation phenotype and implicated in promoting flavonoid-associated metabolic networks under environmental stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AT2G37650 mutation affected seed saturation in Arabidopsis. 2. AT2G37650 formed a close protein interaction network enriched in metabolic process, catalytic activity and binding functions. 3. Transcriptome correlation across 144 accessions showed a robust network between AT2G37650 and directly interacting genes, supporting a regulatory role in metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype mining from TAIR/RARGE-II; co-fractionation mass spectrometry interaction network; accession-scale transcriptome correlation analysis; tissue expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Directly interacting genes in ENOG411DSHQ orthogroup; specific targets NA
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: network-based transcriptional/metabolic regulation; exact direct mode NA
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: flavonoid-associated metabolism and seed trait-associated metabolic processes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate regulator linked to seed saturation and metabolic process networks, but not proven as a master regulator
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: proteins in ENOG411DSHQ orthogroup; 16 fruit-expressed interacting genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Similar expression to 16 interacting genes in fruit
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: environmental stresses analyzed at network level; specific treatment for AT2G37650 NA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation decreases or alters seed saturation; other tradeoffs NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper provides systems-level association rather than direct transactivation evidence for flavonoid control by AT2G37650.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: proteomics (CF-MS); transcriptomics from 144 Arabidopsis accessions GEO: GSE43858; Arabidopsis 1001 Genomes transcriptome GEO: GSE80744; DAP-seq mentioned globally but not assigned specifically to AT2G37650
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: fruit-associated expression similarity with interacting genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Correlated with metabolic and interacting genes; exact activation/repression NA
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Yes; participates in protein interaction network detected by co-fractionation MS
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker/candidate regulator for seed quality and metabolic trait improvement, but requires direct functional validation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids contribute to plant environmental adaptation and stress responses

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GRAS family transcription factor; LISCL subfamily; GRAS domain (PF03514)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with differential flavonoid accumulation between pale-yellow and dark-yellow quinoa fruits and may regulate seed saturation/fruit color-related metabolic differences
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AUR62039362 is a differentially expressed CqGRAS in two quinoa fruit types. 2. It is homologous to Arabidopsis AT2G37650 and forms a close protein interaction network enriched in metabolic process, catalytic activity and binding functions. 3. Only AUR62039362 formed a robust correlation network with other genes in two quinoa fruit types. 4. AUR62039362 and its interacting genes formed a directed regulatory network with flavonoids such as rutin, linking this GRAS to flavonoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; qRT-PCR validation; co-fractionation MS interaction analysis; directed correlation network with metabolites; metabolite profiling of flavonoids/anthocyanins/isoflavonols
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Interacting genes/proteins bound to AUR62039362; core interacting genes enriched in metabolic process and phosphotransferase activity; exact named direct targets NA
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: network-based regulation via crosstalk with auxin signaling, photosynthesis and flavonoid biosynthesis pathways
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: flavonoid biosynthesis through pathway crosstalk; seed/fruit metabolic phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong candidate core regulator because it uniquely forms a robust interaction network in quinoa fruits and is centrally connected to rutin and other flavonoids
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: 31 conserved interacting genes/proteins across pale-yellow and dark-yellow fruits; proteins enriched for catalytic activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differentially expressed in mature fruits between pale-yellow and dark-yellow quinoa; analyzed also in roots, stems, leaves, flowers and fruits
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: comparison of pale-yellow versus dark-yellow mature fruits grown at 25°C, 16 h light/8 h dark
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential effects on seed saturation/fruit phenotype; broader developmental tradeoffs NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is systems/correlation based; direct DNA binding to flavonoid structural genes was not demonstrated in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of quinoa tissues and fruits GEO: GSE139174; proteomics (CF-MS); metabolomics of anthocyanin/flavonoid/isoflavonol
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: auxin signaling; photosynthesis/light-related pathway; environmental adaptation context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Correlated with interacting genes and flavonoid pathway-associated genes; exact activation/repression NA
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Yes; protein interaction network detected by co-fractionation MS
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for improving flavonoid-rich quinoa fruit/seed quality and environmental adaptation, pending direct functional tests
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids support environmental adaptation and stress responses

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GRAS family transcription factor; GRAS domain (PF03514)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate homolog of AT2G37650 that may regulate seed saturation by participating in metabolic pathways; likely functionally redundant with AUR62039362
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AUR62009556 is homologous to AT2G37650 and forms a close interaction network in quinoa. 2. Interacting proteins are enriched in metabolic process, binding and catalytic activity. 3. The paper suggests it may regulate seed saturation and may be functionally redundant with AUR62039362.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: homology inference; co-fractionation MS interaction analysis; GO enrichment of interacting proteins
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Interacting proteins/genes; exact direct targets NA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive candidate regulator, but less decisive than AUR62039362 because only AUR62039362 formed the robust fruit network
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: proteins enriched in metabolic process, binding and catalytic activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core candidate because the Results explicitly analyze it as homologous to AT2G37650, though direct metabolite linkage is weaker than for AUR62039362.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: proteomics (CF-MS); quinoa transcriptomics GEO: GSE139174 context
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Yes; protein interaction network detected by co-fractionation MS
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential backup/redundant target with AUR62039362 for seed quality engineering
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids contribute to plant adaptation to environment

Regulation_Genes.Gene_Name: CqGRASs (12 differentially expressed GRAS genes in quinoa fruits: AUR62017519; AUR62015062; AUR62012823; AUR62017058; AUR62021138; AUR62021833; AUR62031401; AUR62041351; AUR62039362; AUR62009555; AUR62040238; AUR62023581)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GRAS family transcription factors; PAT, LISCL, SHR, and SCL4/7 subfamilies
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Differentially expressed CqGRASs are associated with differential accumulation of flavonoids between pale-yellow and dark-yellow quinoa fruits
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Twelve CqGRAS genes were differentially expressed between two quinoa fruit types. 2. These GRASs were tightly correlated with binding DEGs and differential flavonoid metabolites. 3. They formed close correlation networks with auxin signaling, photosynthesis and flavonoid biosynthesis pathway genes, suggesting regulation of flavonoid synthesis through pathway crosstalk.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling; correlation network analysis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: auxin signaling genes; photosynthesis pathway genes; flavonoid biosynthesis pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: correlation-supported transcriptional regulation through crosstalk among auxin, photosynthesis and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: flavonoid biosynthesis and associated fruit metabolic differentiation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Group-level evidence supports GRASs as core components of flavonoid regulation, but individual direct targets are largely unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: binding DEGs associated with AUR62039362 network; specific genes mostly NA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Differential expression in mature fruits; broader tissue profiles include roots, stems, leaves, flowers and fruits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes systems-level universality of GRAS-flavonoid crosstalk rather than direct validation of each individual DEG.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: auxin; photosynthesis/light-related cues; environmental stress context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Closely correlated with flavonoid biosynthetic, auxin and photosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Partly supported through protein interaction analyses
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate pool for engineering quinoa flavonoid content and adaptation traits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids function in plant environmental adaptation and stress response

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GRAS family transcription factors; GRAS domain (PF03514)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtGRASs are tightly associated with flavonoid biosynthesis pathway genes and may promote flavonoid accumulation under environmental stress through crosstalk with auxin signaling and photosynthesis pathways
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGRASs formed robust correlation networks with photosynthesis, auxin and flavonoid biosynthesis pathway genes under multiple environmental stresses. 2. Most AtGRAS, photosynthesis, auxin and key flavonoid genes were induced under nearly all abiotic stress conditions. 3. The network was validated across 144 natural accessions, the 1001 Genomes dataset and OneKP, supporting a conserved regulatory association with flavonoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stress-responsive transcriptome meta-analysis; population transcriptome correlation analysis; Arabidopsis 1001 Genomes and OneKP comparative network analysis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: photosynthesis pathway genes; auxin signaling genes; key flavonoid biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: The paper positions GRASs as core components of flavonoid biosynthesis-associated networks across species
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under many abiotic stress conditions
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nutrient, mitochondrial/chloroplast stress, hormones, ozone, high light, H2O2, heat, salt, UV, oxidative, osmotic, pathogen/elicitor treatments
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is family-level systems evidence; most individual AtGRAS genes were not directly functionally validated for flavonoid control in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray/RNA-seq stress datasets; 144 accessions GEO: GSE43858; 1001 Genomes GEO: GSE80744; OneKP
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 144 natural accessions and Arabidopsis population resources
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: abiotic stress; hormones including IAA, GA3, ABA, ACC, MEJA, BL, ZT, SA; biotic stress; organellar stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively associated with expression of flavonoid, auxin and photosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Supported generally by CF-MS interaction datasets for some GRAS proteins
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GRAS regulators represent candidate targets for improving stress adaptation and flavonoid-related quality traits in crops
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids support adaptation to environmental stress
